target.title: fix(agents): preserve prompt-released session metadata
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #93193.

Embedded attempts now preserve safe session rows appended while the prompt lock is released without treating them as a takeover or letting a stale `SessionManager` discard them later.

- Accept transcript-only OpenClaw delivery mirrors plus globally resolved `custom`, `label`, and `session_info` entries.
- Merge accepted rows into the active manager as a side branch, retaining them across later whole-file rewrites without changing the prepared reply leaf or adding delivery mirrors to model context.
- Merge both direct fence advances and owned same-process transcript publications.
- Continue rejecting branch-scoped `model_change` and `thinking_level_change` entries, conversational records, malformed rows, and unknown entry types.
- Preserve the supported parentless delivery-mirror append used by legacy linear transcripts larger than 8 MiB.

## Why this shape

Simply allowing more JSONL entry types at the fence is insufficient: the already-open manager remains stale and can later rewrite accepted rows away. Reloading the whole manager is also unsafe because it can drop the prepared or not-yet-flushed active branch. The side-branch merge keeps one canonical in-memory graph, preserves global metadata indexes, and leaves the current reply branch unchanged.

## Verification

Local:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.test.ts
# 37 runtime-config tests + 101 agent tests passed

pnpm check:test-types
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.session-lock.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts src/agents/sessions/session-manager.ts
git diff --check
```

Fresh Codex autoreview completed with no accepted/actionable findings for both the final local patch and the complete branch against `main`.

Crabbox Linux run `run_1664bbd592b4`:

- Node 24.16.0, pnpm 11.2.2
- Production-shaped real-lock E2E passed
- 138 focused tests passed
- `pnpm check:test-types` passed
- full `pnpm build` passed

Run: https://crabbox.openclaw.ai/portal/runs/run_1664bbd592b4

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Prompt-time delivery mirrors and global metadata survive stale-manager appends and later transcript rewrites without entering active model context; branch-scoped model/thinking changes still trigger takeover.

Real environment tested: Linux AWS Crabbox using real session files, `acquireSessionWriteLock`, owned transcript publication, `SessionManager`, and the PR head source.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: A temporary TypeScript scenario created a persisted session, opened a stale manager, released the prompt lock, published a delivery mirror plus a label targeting that mirror and `session_info`, appended the active reply, forced a whole-file rewrite, reopened the session, and separately attempted `model_change` and `thinking_level_change` writes. The same run then executed the focused tests, test typechecks, and full build.

Evidence after fix: Crabbox run `run_1664bbd592b4` exited 0 and printed `PR #93194 Crabbox E2E passed`; all 138 focused tests, test typechecks, and full build passed.

Observed result after fix: The mirror, its label, and session name survived the rewrite; active context contained only the original user/assistant branch plus the current assistant reply; both branch-scoped control changes raised takeover.

What was not tested: A live external channel/provider delivery against production credentials was not exercised; the real on-disk session and lock path used by that delivery was exercised directly.


## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Prompt-time delivery mirrors and global metadata survive stale-manager appends and later transcript rewrites without entering active model context; branch-scoped model/thinking changes still trigger takeover.

Real environment tested: Linux AWS Crabbox using real session files, `acquireSessionWriteLock`, owned transcript publication, `SessionManager`, and the PR head source.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: A Crabbox live scenario created a persisted session, opened a stale manager, released the prompt lock, published a delivery mirror plus a label targeting that mirror and `session_info`, appended the active reply, forced a whole-file rewrite, reopened the session, and separately attempted `model_change` and `thinking_level_change` writes.

Evidence after fix: Terminal output excerpt from Crabbox run `run_1664bbd592b4`:

```text
PR #93194 Crabbox E2E passed
mirror label persisted after rewrite: true
session_info persisted after rewrite: true
active context excludes prompt-time delivery mirror: true
model_change takeover rejected: true
thinking_level_change takeover rejected: true
```

Observed result after fix: The mirror, its label, and session name survived the rewrite; active context contained only the original user/assistant branch plus the current assistant reply; both branch-scoped control changes raised takeover.

What was not tested: A live external channel/provider delivery against production credentials was not exercised; the real on-disk session and lock path used by that delivery was exercised directly.
